creator: eduardo@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Yusuf Yaman from comment #2)

> (...) So we shouldn't specify an already existing destination path.

gitup is supposed to detect if a repository is already been created or not, I've just re-ran command from yesterday ( #Comment 1 ):

# gitup ports
# Scanning local repository...
# Host: git.freebsd.org
# Port: 443
# Repository Path: /ports.git
# Target Directory: /tmp/ports
# Have: 68a7e946601548f7e54a85d7194a3869af72f385
# Want: 20203bded326d3a6d7872d2d6b38fa3c006d3887
# Branch: main
# Action: pull
 * /tmp/ports/databases/R-cran-RPostgreSQL/Makefile
<...>
- /tmp/ports/www/shiori/files
- /tmp/ports/www/shiori/files/patch-internal_database_sqlite__noncgo.go
# Done.

As it should always do, create a full rep when it not exist or update a existing one.

Continue testing and if you find a way to replicate it, please open an gitup upstream PR.

Cheers
